heading: Biennial reports
label: 60124 Biennial reports

L. 96–129, § 213(b)93 Stat. 101460124(b)49 App.:2012(c).In subsection (a), before clause (1), the words “prepare and” and “comprehensive” are omitted as surplus. The words “the following information” are added for clarity. The words “about the prior year” are substituted for “occurring in such year”, “established or in effect in such year”, “during such year”, and “during the preceding calendar year” to eliminate unnecessary words. In clause (2), the word “Federal” is omitted as surplus. The word “prescribed” is substituted for “established or in effect” and “established” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code and to eliminate unnecessary words. The word “newly” is omitted as surplus. In clause (4), the words “for the transportation of gas and pipeline facilities” in 49 App.:1683(a)(4) and “for the transportation of hazardous liquids and pipeline facilities” in 49 App.:2012(a)(4) are omitted because of the restatement. In clause (5), the words “in carrying out” are substituted for “confronting the administration of” for consistency. In clause (9), before subclause (A), the words “by State agencies (including municipalities)” are omitted as surplus. In clauses (9)(B) and (10)(B), the words “in any part” are added for clarity. In clause (10), before subclause (A), the words “with State agencies (including municipalities)” are omitted as surplus. In clause (12), before subclause (A), the word “additional” is omitted as surplus. In subclause (A), the word “several” is omitted as surplus.In subsection (b), the words “annual” and “the report requirements of” are omitted as surplus.

References in TextSection 12 of the Pipeline Safety Improvement Act of 2002, referred to in subsec. (a)(6)(C), is , which is set out as a note under .section 12 of Pub. L. 107–355section 60101 of this title

Amendments2016—Subsec. (a)(6)(C). added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 114–1831996—, substituted “Biennial” for “Annual” in section catchline.Pub. L. 104–304, § 15(a)(1)Subsec. (a). , inserted first sentence and struck out former first sentence which read as follows: “The Secretary of Transportation shall submit to Congress not later than August 15 of each odd-numbered year a report on carrying out this chapter for the prior calendar year for gas and a report on carrying out this chapter for the prior calendar year for hazardous liquid.”Pub. L. 104–304, § 15(a)(2)1995—Subsec. (a). substituted “of each odd-numbered year” for “of each year” in first sentence of introductory provisions.Pub. L. 104–66

Termination of Reporting RequirementsFor termination, effective , of provisions of law requiring submittal to Congress of any annual, semiannual, or other regular periodic report listed in House Document No. 103–7 (in which the 7th and 9th items on page 135 identify reporting provisions which, as subsequently amended, are contained in this section), see , as amended, set out as a note under , Money and Finance.May 15, 2000section 3003 of Pub. L. 104–66section 1113 of Title 31

Termination of Reporting RequirementsFor termination, effective , of provisions of law requiring submittal to Congress of any annual, semiannual, or other regular periodic report listed in House Document No. 103–7 (in which the 7th and 9th items on page 135 identify reporting provisions which, as subsequently amended, are contained in this section), see , as amended, set out as a note under , Money and Finance.May 15, 2000section 3003 of Pub. L. 104–66section 1113 of Title 31

children.text: a thorough compilation of the leak repairs, accidents, and casualties and a statement of cause when investigated and established by the National Transportation Safety Board.
a list of applicable pipeline safety standards prescribed under this chapter including identification of standards prescribed during the year.
a summary of the reasons for each waiver granted under section 60118(c) and (d) of this title.
an evaluation of the degree of compliance with applicable safety standards, including a list of enforcement actions and compromises of alleged violations by location and company name.
a summary of outstanding problems in carrying out this chapter, in order of priority.

children.label: A
children.text: research activities, including their policy implications, completed as a result of the United States Government and private sponsorship;

children.label: B
children.text: technological progress in safety achieved; and

children.label: C
children.text: a summary of each research and development project carried out with Federal and non-Federal entities pursuant to section 12 of the Pipeline Safety Improvement Act of 2002 and a review of how the project affects safety.
a list, with a brief statement of the issues, of completed or pending judicial actions under this chapter.
the extent to which technical information was distributed to the scientific community and consumer-oriented information was made available to the public.

children.label: B
children.text: rejected in any part by the Secretary and a summary of the reasons for each rejection.

children.label: B
children.text: ended in any part by the Secretary and a summary of the reasons for ending each agreement.

a description of the number and qualifications of State pipeline safety inspectors in each State for which a certification under or an agreement under is in effect and the number and qualifications of inspectors the Secretary recommends for that State.section 60105 of this titlesection 60106 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: hazardous liquid pipeline safety programs; and

children.label: B
children.text: to strengthen the national gas pipeline safety program.

children.heading: .—Submission of One Report
children.label: b .—Submission of One Report
children.text: The Secretary may submit one report to carry out subsection (a) of this section.
